title: How to sum times past 24 hours (without them wrapping)
task: Total hours across many rows — and stop the total resetting to zero after 24.

excel.note: The formula is fine — format the result cell as [h]:mm (square brackets stop the 24-hour wrap).

google_sheets.note: Identical; Format → Number → Duration does the same thing.

explanation: 20:00 plus 10:30 is 30 and a half hours, but a cell formatted h:mm shows 6:30 — the display wraps at 24 like a clock, even though the underlying value (1.27 days) is correct. Bracketed hour codes ([h]:mm) tell the app to keep counting instead of wrapping: 30:30. So the fix is formatting, not the SUM. For payroll math, multiply the total by 24 to get decimal hours instead.
